title: ceratostanthus paniculatus
intro: Ceratostanthus paniculatus, also known as the 'Tree Marigold' or 'Pig Face', is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The medicinal properties of Ceratostanthus paniculatus include its use in treating skin conditions and wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving rheumatism, and treating various skin ailments. Ceratostanthus paniculatus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams for its medicinal uses, and its seeds and leaves are often used in traditional medicine.
